component.name: Control Center
component.summary: A modular, pluggable control center for the COSMIC desktop

description.p: A control center for COSMIC, built as a hub of configurable module tiles. Arrange the tiles you want in a roomy editor, then a lean panel applet shows that exact layout in a popup — live and interactive.

ul.li: Volume and Microphone (with mute), Media controls (MPRIS)
Wi-Fi (SSID + link rate), VPN, Bluetooth, Airplane mode
CPU / GPU / RAM gauges, a battery readout, and section dividers

description.p: Early preview (0.1.0): the applet refreshes live module state on a poll, which can briefly stutter while the popup is open — an async pass is planned. The plugin system is functional but experimental.

ul.li: Tile icons now reflect live state: Wi-Fi signal strength, Bluetooth and VPN connection.
The volume tile icon tracks the level (muted / low / medium / high), not just mute.

Optional panel status cluster: the applet button can show a row of live status icons (Wi-Fi/VPN, audio, Bluetooth, power profile) instead of a single icon, configured from a new Settings surface in the editor with a per-indicator show/hide. The cluster is event-driven over D-Bus, with no idle polling.
Fixed the battery readout showing 0% on laptops with wireless peripherals (it now picks the system battery, not a peripheral).
Prefers the GPU (Vulkan) backend when available instead of CPU software rendering, and the network list rows are lighter to draw.

description.p: Inline Wi-Fi/Bluetooth/VPN selection from the tiles (connect to networks, devices, and profiles, including new secured Wi-Fi); a right-hand config sidebar for per-tile settings; the media tile's style (COSMIC or album art) is now a per-tile option; the add-module list is grouped into categories with single-instance modules; the control center follows the system accent colour; and module data is fetched off the UI thread for a smoother popup.

Bug fixes: system monitor gauges now render correctly in the panel applet popup; the power profile icon and the volume/microphone sliders display correctly in the editor; the editor is single-instance and the applet gear focuses the open editor instead of opening duplicates.
First public preview: modular control-center editor + panel applet, a suite of built-in modules, light/dark theming, and a declarative RON plugin system.
